title: DoorDash to Lay Off 1,250 Employees
text: The food delivery service is the latest tech company to cut staff.

title: DoorDash Layoffs 2022: What to Know About the Latest DASH Job Cuts
text: Food delivery company DoorDash (NYSE: DASH ) announced it will be laying off about 6% of its workforce, equivalent to roughly 1,250 employees. What do DoorDash layoffs mean for the company?

title: DoorDash is laying off 6% of its staff, and it's a warning for the entire food delivery business
text: Food delivery giant, DoorDash, says it will continue to search for ways to cut costs after laying off over 1,000 employees.

title: DoorDash to lay off 1,250 corporate employees
text: DoorDash on Wednesday said it will lay off about 1,250 corporate employees after growing its team too quickly during the pandemic, making it the latest tech company to cut staff in recent weeks.

title: DoorDash cuts 1,250 jobs as deliveries ebb after pandemic
text: DoorDash is eliminating more than 1,200 corporate jobs, saying it hired too many people when delivery demand surged during the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: DoorDash lays off 1,250 employees to cut costs
text: DoorDash announced that it will lay off more than 1,200 employees in order to reduce operating expenses that, "if left unabated – would continue to outgrow our revenue."

title: DoorDash lays off 1,250 employees to rein in operating expenses
text: DoorDash is laying off 1,250 people in an effort to rein in costs, the company's CEO Tony Xu said in a message to employees on Wednesday. Xu's message notes that the pandemic presented unprecedented opportunities to serve merchants and consumers, and as a result, DoorDash sped up hiring to catch up with growth.

title: DoorDash Grew Too Fast. It's Cutting 1,250 Jobs.
text: The food-delivery company joins the line of tech companies, like Amazon, Meta, and Twitter, making staff cuts.

title: DoorDash lays off 1,250 employees
text: Doordash became the latest tech company to lay off staff, with CEO Tony Xu announcing that over 1200 workers would be laid off as part of broad cost cuts.

title: Doordash to lay off 1,250 employees as CEO says costs grew too fast
text: Food-delivery platform DoorDash Inc. plans to lay off about 1,250 employees, becoming the latest technology company to admit that it grew expenses too rapidly for the current climate.

title: DoorDash Cuts Staff by 1,250 to Rein in Costs
text: Growth has tapered from pandemic highs, the chief executive said in a memo to staff, and operating costs would continue to outpace sales growth if left unaddressed.

title: Don't Skip DoorDash's Side Dishes
text: Investors are finally excited about growth again, but not just in restaurant delivery.

title: NYC proposes $24 minimum wage for app-based delivery drivers
text: A little over a year after the New York City Council approved a legislative package aimed at improving working conditions for app-based delivery drivers, city officials are following through on one of the legislation's key provisions. Uber Eats (NYSE: UBER), DoorDash (NYSE: DASH) and Grubhub (OCTUS: JTKWY) drivers in the Big Apple could earn a minimum wage of nearly $24 an hour by 2025 if the city approves a proposal passed down by the Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP) on Wednesday.

title: DoorDash rolls out new safety features for delivery people on its platform
text: DoorDash is rolling out new in-app safety features to help ensure delivery people on its platform are safe before, during and after every order. Now, if DoorDash detects that a delivery is taking longer than expected, the app will automatically check to see if the delivery person is okay.

title: This startup just hired a former DoorDash exec to bring robotic restaurant delivery to the masses
text: Serve Robotics wants to entice chains like The Cheesecake Factory and Taco Bell to deliver food with its sidewalk robots.

title: DoorDash Outperforms Despite Losses - Is It Time To Buy?
text: DoorDash has not done too well since its IPO but in a couple years, sales have improved greatly. Don't let sluggish earnings deter you from a potential buy.

title: Wing brings drone delivery options to DoorDash customers in Logan, Australia
text: DoorDash is teaming up with Alphabet's Wing to offer customers an easier way to arrange for goods to be delivered via drone.

title: Analyst Upgrades DoorDash Stock on Improving Margins
text: The shares of DoorDash Inc (NYSE:DASH) are down 0.6% at $51.20 this morning, despite a bull note from Oppenheimer.

title: DoorDash Stock: Bear vs. Bull
text: The food delivery leader remains a divisive investment.

title: DoorDash (DASH) Reports Q3 Loss, Beats Revenue Estimates
text: DoorDash (DASH) third-quarter 2022 results reflect the negative impacts of drastic research and development and general & administrative cost hikes.

title: Down 79%, DoorDash Stock Looks Cheap After Beat And Raise
text: Despite expectations-beating growth and guidance and record orders in the most recent quarter, DoorDash stock has lost 79% of its value. That could make its shares a bargain.

title: Jobs report, Twilio plunge, DoorDash rise and more: Friday's 5 things to know
text: It is jobs day where traders will be looking to see if interest rate hikes by the Fed is slowing the pace of hiring.

title: DoorDash, Inc. (DASH) Reports Q3 Loss, Tops Revenue Estimates
text: DoorDash, Inc. (DASH) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -28.33% and 3.71%, respectively, for the quarter ended September 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: DoorDash Stock Jumps As Quarterly Results Beat Estimates On Revenue
text: DoorDash stock jumped as the food delivery company reported quarterly results that missed on earnings but beat on revenue. The post DoorDash Stock Jumps As Quarterly Results Beat Estimates On Revenue appeared first on Investor's Business Daily.

title: DoorDash revenue beats estimates as appetite for food delivery holds up
text: Food delivery company DoorDash Inc said orders surged to a record high in the third quarter as people stuck to their pandemic-era habits despite rising inflation and steeper prices, helping it beat Wall Street targets for revenue.

title: DoorDash analysts remain optimistic as the company expands from delivering restaurant orders to delivering everything
text: Ahead of this week's earnings, analysts say DoorDash is building momentum in grocery and retail delivery. DashPass members give the app an advantage.
